# KakaoTalk gifting service used nearly 200 mln times this year

Published : Dec. 25, 2025 - 13:39:40

- - Link copied!



![One of the features of KakaoTalk, Korea's most popular instand messaging service, include "Kakao Gift" which allows users to conveniently send coupons or gifts directly through the chat. (Source: Kakao Commerce)](https://wimg.heraldcorp.com/news/cms/2025/12/25/news-p.v1.20251225.e9cb9d019b6e48ad91a3a1266c5e4c58_P1.jpg)One of the features of KakaoTalk, Korea's most popular instand messaging service, include "Kakao Gift" which allows users to conveniently send coupons or gifts directly through the chat. (Source: Kakao Commerce)

KakaoTalk's gifting service was used nearly 200 million times this year, Kakao Corp. said Thursday.

Data from the company showed approximately 189 million gifts were sent through Dec. 17, translating to an average of 540,000 gifts per day.

The service offers 640,000 types of gifts from some 8,700 brands.

The most popular item was a Starbucks gift certificate, followed by certificates from leading food delivery application Baemin, discount retailer Emart and beauty chain Olive Young.

One notable trend was users buying gifts for themselves. Popular items in that category included luxury cosmetic brands Dior and Prada, as well as British home appliance maker Dyson, which ranked No. 1, 6 and 3, respectively.

Beauty gift certificates ranked first on "Wish Lists," showing users favor practical gifts that let recipients choose based on personal tastes.

The gifting service was most heavily used on Nov. 11, widely known here as Pepero Day, the same as last year, followed by Valentine's Day and Teachers' Day.

The company said data reveals a trend toward respecting individual preferences, with "practicality," "personal taste" and "experience" emerging as key themes.

web.title: Survey Reveals Gift Preferences for Parents' Day in South Korea
web.description: Gift cards and e-coupons emerged as the most desired gifts for Parents' Day, excluding cash, with 22% of respondents favoring them.
web.markdown:
[![](http://cache-02.cleanprint.net/media/pfviewer/images/CleanPrintBtn_text_small.png)](http://koreabizwire.com/survey-reveals-gift-preferences-for-parents-day-in-south-korea/ "Print page")

![Parents' day gifts (Image courtesy of Asung Daiso)](http://koreabizwire.com/wp/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/1042906_1065403_1221.jpg)

Parents’ day gifts (Image courtesy of Asung Daiso)

**SEOUL, Apr. 24 (Korea Bizwire) –** According to a survey conducted by the e-commerce platform Tmon from April 15 to 21, involving over 500 customers, gift cards and e-coupons emerged as the most desired gifts for Parents’ Day, excluding cash, with 22% of respondents favoring them.

Fresh produce, such as fruits and meat, secured the second spot at 16%, while a heartfelt expression like “I love you” unexpectedly ranked third with 11% of responses.

Other popular gift choices included health supplements and domestic travel or cultural experiences (tied at 10%), followed by home appliances, digital products, and overseas travel (both at 9%).

When asked about the gifts respondents would like to give, excluding cash, health supplements topped the list at 24%, closely followed by fresh produce (23%), gift cards and e-coupons (15%), and domestic travel or cultural experiences (10%).

Regarding their budget for Parents’ Day gifts, nearly half (44%) of the respondents planned to spend between 100,000 and 200,000 won. A significant portion also indicated budgets ranging from 50,000 to 100,000 won (28%) and 200,000 to 300,000 won (12%).

When it came to purchase channels, 61% of respondents favored online platforms for their gift-buying needs.

Tmon announced that it would host a special “Family Month” promotion until May 12, featuring over 700 popular gift items tailored for Parents’ Day and Children’s Day.

“With an increasing number of customers preparing for Family Month, we have curated a robust product lineup and attractive promotions,” said Jun Googyung, the head of Tmon’s marketing division.

Lina Jang (linajang@koreabizwire.com)

web.description: Chocolate sales in South Korea are 100% higher today than they were a decade ago and the market continues to grow.

![South Korea Chocolate Consumption Increases 100% on Pepero Day](https://media.licdn.com/dms/image/v2/C5112AQHbDI8UX7NJbg/article-cover_image-shrink_720_1280/article-cover_image-shrink_720_1280/0/1520186309880?e=2147483647&v=beta&t=KwmaVljB1m4V5x6gIgfNHGLIxMuF3tm57sVip0JKuwQ)

South Korea has several days in the year when relationships are celebrated. There is Valentine’s Day, which is on February the 14th just as in most other countries, but South Koreans have three more celebration days. There is White Day on March 14, Black Day on April 14, and coming soon there is Pepero Day on November 11.

On Valentine’s Day it is traditional for girls to give boys a chocolate gift. On White Day boys will give candy to girls. On Black Day single people will celebrate by eating black noodles called _jajagmyeon._

However, on Pepero Day couples will exchange chocolates called Pepero - it’s like a chocolate covered cookie. The number 11 looks a little like the Pepero stick, hence the day is on 11.11 each year. Often people will try to eat the chocolate at 11am on the 11th of November.

What is interesting to observe is that chocolate sales in South Korea are 100% higher today than they were a decade ago and the market continues to grow. Pepero Day has played a significant role in the development of the chocolate market in South Korea although it was not a tradition created by the company.

Nobody can determine exactly when Pepero Day started, although there are many stories that date from 1983. It was informally popular throughout the 1980s and since 1997 the manufacturer of Pepero, Lotte, has officially promoted the event.

Some have criticised Lotte for promoting an increase in chocolate consumption, rather than food that is considered to be healthier, but the company has defended their snacks and the event itself. A treat now and then can be beneficial for everybody!

I think that the creation of this tradition shows a very interesting approach to the customer experience of chocolate consumption in South Korea. Customers started this tradition because of the shape of the chocolate snacks yet it has become internationally known as a celebration day because the brand encouraged customers to celebrate their product. Imagine if your customers did not just buy your products, but celebrated with them and created a special day too!

Pepero day is just one of the many events that we study here in Teleperformance, if we see that it will aid our clients. Teleperformance and our experience in analysing market trends and consumer behaviors, and translating them into the creation of better customer experience and the improvement of services we provide based on the dynamics of the consumer population and market demands ensure that we are maximizing every learning and business opportunity to further the goal and objectives of our clients. Such is the inspiration in the creation of our Customer Experience Lab.

# Average 600,000 online gifts exchanged daily on Kakao Talk

Button LabelListen

Text Size

Print

- PublishedDec 22, 2024 3:31 pmKST
- UpdatedDec 22, 2024 3:31 pmKST

![Customer purchases gift through KakaoTalk / Provided by Lotte Shopping](https://newsimg.koreatimes.co.kr/2024/12/22/353de11d-829b-42c2-b5a9-200dcf1b25aa.jpg?w=728)

Customer purchases gift through KakaoTalk / Provided by Lotte Shopping

An average of 600,000 gifts are exchanged daily via Kakao Talk, Korea's largest messaging app, its operator said Sunday, with coffee gift vouchers and vitamins topping the list.

According to data from Kakao Talk, compiled between December 2023 and November 2024, the most number of gifts were exchanged on Pepero Day, a day when friends and lovers exchange chocolate-covered sticks on Nov. 11.

Spikes in online gifting were also seen on Valentine's Day, days prior to the Lunar New Year holiday and one day before the annual college entrance exam, according to Kakao, the operator of the messaging app.

The company said the figures reflected how Kakao Talk has "deeply" placed itself as a major means of gift giving in the digital environment.

Gift items high on people's wish lists included wireless earphones, Korean beef gift sets, lip gloss and gift cards, as well as certificates for beverages sold at convenience stores.

Among gift vouchers, those for cafes, coffee and other beverages topped the list, followed by coupons for chicken, late-night snacks, and those from department stores and large grocery stores.

As for delivered gifts, vitamins sat on the top of the list, while hand creams, lip balms, cakes and chocolate followed next.

The company said 660,000 types of goods from some 8,600 brands have been available through its gifting service this year.

Meanwhile, Kakao has been operating an artificial intelligence-equipped shopping mate ahead of Christmas, offering recommendations for trendy, customized gifts and notifying users of their friends' birthdays in advance. (Yonhap)
